behaviors.description: Process files created in subdirectories under the watched root.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior plus daemon path filtering. MOCK-MISS: bug appears at watcher->pipeline handoff for recursive paths, not just pure function logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can inject a subdirectory path directly and miss real watcher recursion/config issues; real OS events are needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt` via actual filesystem write, and assert one transformed output is produced. This requires real watcher event delivery for nested paths, so System is necessary.

behaviors.description: Discard duplicate processing requests for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local module interaction (event intake + dedupe state + scheduler), not OS primitives. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit of dedupe map logic may miss clock/config wiring bugs, so Unit is too low. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess for this behavior text, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about 'deduplication logic' for same path/time window, which can be exercised by in-process requests and controllable clock.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake handler, dedupe component, and processing dispatcher together in-process with a fake clock; submit two requests for identical path within the configured window and assert only one dispatch, then advance clock past window and assert dispatch occurs again. This catches real handoff/state bugs without requiring OS events.

behaviors.description: Process exactly once when OS emits multiple filesystem events for one file write.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS duplicate event patterns. REAL-INFRA: requires real watcher + OS event emission characteristics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked single event (or deterministic duplicates) can miss true event burst timing/coalescing failures. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory, perform a single file write pattern known to trigger multiple watcher events (e.g., write+rename/flush), and assert one pipeline execution/output for that file. Exact failure only appears with real filesystem events.

behaviors.description: Resume watching after watched directory is deleted and recreated while daemon stays running.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS filesystem lifecycle (inode/path invalidation, watcher rebind). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks usually do not reproduce delete/recreate watcher invalidation semantics. No LLM aspects.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory, delete watched dir, recreate it at same path, then create a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon. This must use real filesystem operations and watcher behavior.

behaviors.description: When many files arrive simultaneously, processing follows creation-timestamp order.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on timestamp source and arrival race from real filesystem events. MOCK-MISS: unit/integration can verify sorting logic but miss real timestamp granularity/tie/race behavior at watcher->queue boundary. REAL-INFRA: needed to catch true concurrent arrival behavior. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files in a real watched directory near-simultaneously, capture processing order from daemon logs/hooks, and assert it matches creation timestamps (with explicit tie-break rule if equal). This validates real event/timestamp interaction, not just comparator logic.

behaviors.description: Do not process a file until writing is complete if arrival event fires mid-write.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about real partial-write timing and filesystem event sequencing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked writes/events usually make completion deterministic and can hide premature pickup bugs. No LLM or multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, write a file in chunks with delays (keeping handle open), trigger arrival early, and assert processing starts only after final close/settle condition; verify output uses full final content. Requires real file I/O timing and watcher events.
